navigation_overview:
PHP Travels is a comprehensive travel booking platform. The top navigation bar features links for Home, Hotels, Flights, Tours, Cars, Visa, Offers, Blog, and Account/Login, along with a currency selector and language selector on the right side. After successful authentication, the Login/Signup option is replaced by the user's name with a dropdown providing access to the dashboard and account management.

---

module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can initiate searches and switch between service tabs (Hotels, Flights, Tours, Cars) and enter search criteria.
Line 2: Shows the active search tab and current search parameters (destination/cities, dates, guests/rooms, trip type, cabin class).

Line 1: Users can enter registration details, accept terms and submit to create an account.
Line 2: Shows form fields, inline validation errors, and success/verification prompts (account created or email verification required).

Line 1: Users can log in with credentials or social providers, toggle Remember Me, and trigger password recovery or CAPTCHA if required.
Line 2: Shows credential fields, login errors, social login options, CAPTCHA challenge state, and redirection result on success.

Line 1: Users can request a password reset by submitting their email and set a new password via the reset link.
Line 2: Shows the entered email field, confirmation or error messages, reset link status and reset form fields for new password entry.

Line 1: Users can search for hotels, apply sidebar filters and sort results, and start a booking via Book Now.
Line 2: Shows hotel search results as cards with name, location, star rating, thumbnail, starting price, amenities and active filters.

Line 1: Users can view hotel details, select room types and complete a booking by entering guest and contact information.
Line 2: Shows photo gallery, full property description, map, room types with pricing/occupancy, reviews/ratings, policies and a booking price breakdown.

Line 1: Users can search for flights (one-way/round-trip/multi-city), filter/sort results, expand details and select a flight.
Line 2: Shows flight results with airline, departure/arrival times and airports, duration, stops, and price per passenger plus expanded segment details.

Line 1: Users can enter passenger and contact details, select seats/meals and submit the flight booking for payment.
Line 2: Shows full itinerary summary, passenger count and details form, cabin class, and a fare breakdown (base fare, taxes, fees, totals) with validation feedback.

Line 1: Users can search and filter tours by destination, dates, type, duration and budget, then proceed to book a tour.
Line 2: Shows tour listing cards with image, name, destination, duration, starting price per person, availability status and traveler rating.

Line 1: Users can review detailed tour itineraries, select a departure date and traveler counts, then book by providing traveler and contact details.
Line 2: Shows slideshow, day-by-day itinerary, inclusions/exclusions, available departure dates, per-person pricing, map, reviews and terms/conditions.

Line 1: Users can search for rental cars, set pickup/dropoff parameters and filter by category, then initiate a booking.
Line 2: Shows vehicle listings grouped by category with images, make/model, transmission, fuel policy, seating/luggage capacity, feature icons, price per day and total cost for the period.

Line 1: Users can enter driver details, choose insurance and add-ons, review pricing and complete a car booking.
Line 2: Shows selected vehicle details, rental period, pickup/dropoff locations and times, and a full pricing breakdown including daily rates, taxes, insurance and add-on costs.

Line 1: Users can select nationality and destination to view visa requirements and submit a visa application with document uploads.
Line 2: Shows visa type, processing time, required documents, fees, the visa application form and uploaded document previews/status.

Line 1: Users can view and manage bookings (view, download confirmations, cancel or modify when allowed), edit profile, and manage wallet/credits.
Line 2: Shows a consolidated bookings list (past and upcoming) with reference, service type, travel dates, status, profile information and wallet/credits balance.

Line 1: Users can view full booking details and perform permitted modifications (change dates, update traveler info, add requests) or cancel with confirmation.
Line 2: Shows confirmation number, full service information, traveler details, payment info, current booking status and calculated refund/cancellation amounts and policies.

Line 1: Users can choose a payment method, enter card or alternative payment details, and submit payment to complete a booking.
Line 2: Shows full booking summary, price breakdown (base, taxes, fees, discounts, total), supported payment methods and security/SSL indicators.

Line 1: Users can change the display currency and the site language, with preferences saved for authenticated users or stored in session/cookies.
Line 2: Shows prices updated to the selected currency and the site interface translated to the chosen language for the current session or user profile.

Line 1: Users can apply and adjust filters and sorting controls on listing pages to refine search results.
Line 2: Shows the results grid with active filter settings, price/rating sliders and listing-specific filter options relevant to the service type.

Line 1: Users can read and filter reviews and, if eligible, submit reviews with ratings and photos.
Line 2: Shows aggregate rating and total review count, individual reviews with overall and category ratings, reviewer details, dates, comments and uploaded photos.

Line 1: Users can browse promotional offers, filter deals by service/destination/dates and click Book Now to apply or use a promo in booking.
Line 2: Shows promotional banners and deal cards with title, image, discount/special rate, validity period, T&C link and Book Now action.

Line 1: Users can log out to terminate their session and clear sensitive session data.
Line 2: Shows the post-logout redirect (home page) and enforces protected-page redirects to the login page for unauthenticated access attempts.

test_cases.title: Post-logout redirect to login when revisiting an authenticated page
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in (use a normal user account).
steps: Log in with valid credentials on the Login page.
Navigate to a protected page (e.g., User Dashboard) and confirm authenticated content is visible.
Click Logout to end the session.
Attempt to navigate to the protected page again (use the same link or direct navigation).
test_cases.expected_result: After logout, navigating to the previously-open authenticated page redirects to the Login page (no authenticated content is shown).

test_cases.title: Browser back button after logout does not restore authenticated content
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in.
steps: Log in with valid credentials and open a protected page (e.g., Booking Management).
Click Logout to end the session.
Use the browser Back button to return to the previously viewed page.
test_cases.expected_result: Using the Back button does not display cached authenticated content; the page either shows the login screen or a notice that the session has ended.

test_cases.title: Session expiry during form entry forces re-authentication or shows expiry notice
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in; tester can fill a booking form.
steps: Log in with valid credentials and navigate to a protected form page (e.g., Flight Booking).
Begin filling the form but do not submit.
Simulate session expiry (wait beyond the application's session timeout or delete the session cookie) and then attempt to submit the form or interact further with the page.
test_cases.expected_result: On submission or further interaction after session expiry, the application either displays a session-expired notice and redirects to Login, or requires re-authentication before accepting the action; no data submission should proceed as an authenticated request.

test_cases.title: CAPTCHA or lockout enforced after repeated failed login attempts
test_cases.preconditions: Tester can reach the Login page.
steps: Open the Login page and enter a valid username with an incorrect password multiple times in a row (repeat failed attempts according to application policy, e.g., 3–5 attempts).
After the configured threshold, attempt another login.
test_cases.expected_result: After repeated failed attempts the application enforces additional protection (a CAPTCHA appears or further attempts are locked/blocked) and prevents unrestricted login attempts until the protection requirement is satisfied.

test_cases.title: After password change the old password no longer authenticates
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and knows the current password; tester can access the account settings page to change the password.
steps: Log in with the current password, navigate to the account settings or change-password page, and change the password to a new value.
Log out, then attempt to log in using the old password and verify the result; finally, log in with the new password to confirm it works.
test_cases.expected_result: Logging in with the old password fails after the change; logging in with the new password succeeds, confirming the old credentials were invalidated.

test_cases.title: Social login options (Google, Facebook) are displayed when enabled
test_cases.preconditions: Social login options (Google and Facebook) are enabled in system settings
steps: Observe the login form area on the page
Verify that Google and Facebook social login options are displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Google and Facebook social login options are visible on the login page.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None

Click "Login"

test_cases.title: Page refresh on authenticated page keeps the user logged in
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in.
steps: Log in with valid credentials and open a protected page (e.g., User Dashboard).
Click the browser Refresh/Reload button.
Observe the page and any authenticated UI elements after reload.
test_cases.expected_result: After refresh, the user remains authenticated and protected content and user-specific UI remain visible (no redirect to login).

test_cases.title: Remember Me checked persists session after closing and reopening the browser
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can access the Login page.
steps: Navigate to the Login page, enter valid credentials, check the 'Remember Me' checkbox, and submit to log in.
Close all browser windows completely, then reopen the browser and navigate back to the application without logging in again.
test_cases.expected_result: After reopening the browser, the tester remains authenticated (or is still able to access protected pages without re-entering credentials), indicating the session persisted when 'Remember Me' was checked.

test_cases.title: Remember Me unchecked does not persist session after closing and reopening the browser
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can access the Login page.
steps: Navigate to the Login page, enter valid credentials, ensure 'Remember Me' is unchecked, and submit to log in.
Close all browser windows completely, then reopen the browser and navigate back to the application.
test_cases.expected_result: After reopening the browser, the user is not still authenticated and is presented the Login page (session did not persist when 'Remember Me' was unchecked).

test_cases.title: Expired password-reset link shows an appropriate error
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has access to an account email inbox to receive password reset links.
steps: On the Login page, use Forgot Password to request a reset for a valid account and capture the reset link from the received email.
Wait until the application's stated reset-link expiry period has passed, then open the captured reset link in the browser.
test_cases.expected_result: Opening an expired password-reset link shows a clear error message indicating the link has expired and prevents resetting the password using that link.

test_cases.title: Forgot password shows error for unregistered email (no silent success)
test_cases.preconditions: Tester can reach the Forgot Password form.
steps: Open the Forgot Password page, enter an email address that is not registered in the system, and submit the request.
Observe the response shown on the page.
test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a clear error or message indicating the email is not registered (not a silent success), so the tester understands the address is not in the system.
